act-andrew-barr-kurrajong.summary: In the ACT Legislative Assembly debate on the Discrimination Amendment Bill 2022 (23 March 2023), Barr spoke in support of the bill, which narrowed exceptions for religious bodies so that, when providing public goods, services or employment, they may discriminate only on the ground of a person's religious conviction and not on other protected attributes such as sexuality, gender identity or marital status, while leaving greater freedom for religious observances such as the training and ordination of clergy. This is a stated position and a recorded contribution to debate.

act-andrew-braddock-yerrabi.summary: In the Discrimination Amendment Bill 2022 debate (23 March 2023), Braddock (Greens) spoke in support of the bill, applauding the refinement of the Act's exceptions (including those affecting religious bodies) so that discrimination must be reasonable, proportionate and justifiable, and stated the Greens would oppose the Opposition's amendment that he said would effectively restore a blanket exemption for clubs to discriminate. This is a stated position and a recorded contribution to debate.

act-ed-cocks-murrumbidgee.summary: In a Canberra Daily profile, Cocks listed 'freedom of worship' among the Liberal values he personally identifies with, naming it alongside freedom of speech, freedom of thought, freedom to own property and freedom to make choices in the market. This is a stated position.

sources.title: From public service to politics: Ed Cocks MLA

act-elizabeth-lee-kurrajong.summary: In the Discrimination Amendment Bill 2022 debate (23 March 2023), amendments circulated in Elizabeth Lee's name (moved on her behalf as she was absent) proposed inserting an objects clause stating the indivisibility and universality of human rights and that every person is free and equal in dignity, an extended commencement period, and a club/voluntary-body exception; the objects-clause amendment was put to a division and negatived 7-14. These are recorded proposed amendments attributed to her.

act-jeremy-hanson-murrumbidgee.summary: In the Discrimination Amendment Bill 2022 debate (23 March 2023), Hanson said the Canberra Liberals would support the bill, stated that people should be protected from discrimination on the basis of their faith as well as other attributes, noted faith groups remained 'nervous' about possible unintended consequences, and moved amendments (including on behalf of Elizabeth Lee) seeking a longer commencement period and an objects clause asserting that all human rights are held with equal importance. This reflects stated positions and recorded contributions to debate.

act-tara-cheyne-ginninderra.summary: As Minister for Human Rights, Cheyne introduced and championed the Discrimination Amendment Bill 2022; in the debate (23 March 2023) she stated the bill amended the exceptions for religious bodies to 'strike a better balance between the right to equality and the right to freedom of thought, conscience, religion and belief,' citing examples such as prayer groups still being able to exclude people who do not share the relevant religious conviction. This is a stated position and a recorded contribution to debate.
